name: Hot Dog and Hamburger Buns

ingredients.item: Toppings: sesame seeds or poppy seeds

method: Using nonstick cooking spray, coat a large mixing bowl.
Combine lukewarm milk and 1 teaspoon sugar in a glass
measuring cup or small bowl. Sprinkle yeast on top and set
aside about 5 minutes, or until frothy. To dissolve the yeast, stir
it in.
Combine flour, remaining tablespoon sugar, and salt in the bowl
of a stand mixer equipped with a dough hook. In a mixing dish,
combine the yeast and water. While the mixer is running, beat
on medium speed and add butter 1 tablespoon at a time, making
sure each piece is well incorporated before adding the next.
Continue mixing for at least 5 minutes or until a smooth dough
develops—place dough in a greased mixing basin. Cover with a
kitchen towel and set aside for 1 hour to double in size.
Deflate the dough by punching it down and turning it out onto a
lightly floured board. Divide the mixture into 12 equal portions.
Using parchment paper, line a large baking sheet.
Roll each piece of dough into a ball and flatten it into a 3" circle
to make hamburger buns. Place about 12" apart on a baking
sheet that has been prepped.
To create hot dog buns, stretch each piece of dough into a 4 12"
long, 2" broad cylinder. Place about 12" apart on a baking sheet
that has been prepped.
Cover the dough and set it for another 45 minutes to rise.
Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit. Brush the buns with
egg wash and, if preferred, sprinkle with sesame seeds or poppy
seeds.
Bake for 20 minutes, or until brown.
